content.problemStatement:
You have been given an integer KKK and four arrays AAA, BBB, CCC, and DDD with sizes SAS_ASA​, SBS_BSB​, SCS_CSC​ and SDS_DSD​ respectively.

Follow the given steps:


Pick four indices xxx, yyy, zzz, and www from arrays AAA, BBB, CCC, and DDD respectively. Note that (1≤x≤SA)(1 \le x \le S_A)(1≤x≤SA​), (1≤y≤SB)(1 \le y \le S_B)(1≤y≤SB​), (1≤z≤SC)(1 \le z \le S_C)(1≤z≤SC​) and (1≤w≤SD)(1 \le w \le S_D)(1≤w≤SD​).
There are in total SA×SB×SC×SDS_A\times S_B\times S_C\times S_DSA​×SB​×SC​×SD​ ways to do the above step. For each way, write down the value of (Ax+By)×(Cz+Dw)(A_x + B_y) \times (C_z + D_w)(Ax​+By​)×(Cz​+Dw​) on a board.
Sort all the numbers written on the board in **non-decreasing** order.
Find the KthK^{th}Kth element written on the board.


Input Format

First line contains five integers SAS_ASA​, SBS_BSB​, SCS_CSC​, SDS_DSD​ and KKK.
The second line contains SAS_ASA​ space-separated integers A1,A2,...,ASAA_1,A_2,...,A_{S_A}A1​,A2​,...,ASA​​ denoting the array AAA.
The third line contains SBS_BSB​ space-separated integers B1,B2,...,BSBB_1,B_2,...,B_{S_B}B1​,B2​,...,BSB​​ denoting the array BBB.
The fourth line contains SCS_CSC​ space-separated integers C1,C2,...,CSCC_1,C_2,...,C_{S_C}C1​,C2​,...,CSC​​ denoting the array CCC.
The fifth line contains SDS_DSD​ space-separated integers D1,D2,...,DSDD_1,D_2,...,D_{S_D}D1​,D2​,...,DSD​​ denoting the array DDD.


Output Format
For each test case, output the required number in a single line .

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
After sorting the numbers on the board are in the following order {8,8,10,10,12,15,16,16,20,20,20,25}\{8, 8, 10, 10, 12, 15, 16, 16, 20, 20, 20, 25\}{8,8,10,10,12,15,16,16,20,20,20,25}. So, the 10th10^{th}10th number is 202020.
